author: Dr. Steven Lowe (PCP)
content:
PRIMARY CARE VISIT — SOUTH HILLS INTERNAL MEDICINE
Date: 2/15/2027

SUBJECTIVE: 55F with known alcohol-related cirrhosis reports fatigue, decreased appetite, and some unintentional weight loss over the past several weeks. Continues to drink alcohol. Socially isolated; limited family contact (estranged from her adult children). 

OBJECTIVE: Thin. Mild temporal wasting. Abdomen soft, no large ascites today.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
#Unintentional weight loss / poor appetite in cirrhosis — early; will monitor and obtain labs; refer back to hepatology for evaluation and to address the weight trajectory.
#Alcohol use disorder — counseled on cessation; offered resources.
#Cirrhosis — continue hepatology follow-up; continue lactulose/rifaximin.
Follow-up with hepatology arranged.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Steven Lowe, MD. 2/15/2027.
